item.title: "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" guests for June 7, 2026
item.description: This week's guests include Democratic Rep. Jim Himes and Republican Rep. Don Bacon.

item.title: Employers added 172,000 jobs in May, surging past expectations
item.description: The labor market continues to show strength despite rising inflation and concerns about slowing economic growth.

item.title: Graham Platner faces another controversy days ahead of Maine Senate primary
item.description: This week, the New York Times reported allegations of Platner's "unsettling" behavior toward women he dated, including one claim that he was physically abusive, which Platner denies.

item.title: Colombia's Petro accuses Trump of intervening in his country's election
item.description: Colombian President Gustavo Petro argues the U.S. has chosen to align against his government and back forces he identifies as complicit in the drug trade.

item.title: Marine veteran held at gunpoint fights off his attackers
item.description: A Marine veteran was working on his truck in front of his home in Oxon Hill, Maryland, this week, when four teens tried to rob him at gunpoint. That is when his military training kicked in. Tom Hanson reports.

item.title: Despite positive jobs report, rising costs leave many U.S. farmers struggling
item.description: U.S. employers added 172,000 jobs in May, according to the Labor Department, a better-than-expected jobs report. But in the heart of Wisconsin's farm country, many farmers are facing a different reality as they struggle with tariffs and rising fuel and fertilizer costs amid the Iran war. Nikole Killion has more.

item.title: Adviser to Iran's supreme leader says "ball is in Trump's court"
item.description: It remains unclear how close the U.S. and Iran are to striking a deal to end the Iran war amid continued hostilities between Israel and Iran-backed Hezbollah.

item.title: Controversy swirls around Graham Platner ahead of Maine Senate primary
item.description: Just days to go before the Senate primary in Maine, Democratic candidate Graham Platner insists he won't drop out of the race despite a new report from The New York Times in which three women who dated Platner detailed behavior they found "unsettling." It's the latest issue facing the fledgling political hopeful. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: State Department on World Cup safety preparations
item.description: The World Cup starts next week, hosted by the U.S., Canada and Mexico. Millions from around the world will travel to watch them. State Department deputy spokesperson Mignon Houston joins "The Takeout" to discuss the plan.

item.title: No bags, no watch parties: What to expect when Trump attends NBA Finals
item.description: As President Trump prepares to watch the New York Knicks take on the San Antonio Spurs at Madison Square Garden, officials are planning for a heightened security posture, sources said.

item.title: International Space Station crew moves to "safe haven" amid leak concerns
item.description: Five astronauts were briefly forced to take shelter, or "safe haven," while new air leaks were being repaired on the International Space Station. The astronauts boarded a spacecraft attached to the ISS while two cosmonauts assessed the situation. The crew resumed normal duties about two hours later. CBS News space consultant Bill Harwood has more details.

item.title: Park ranger dies after falling into crevasse on America's tallest peak
item.description: The National Park Service said a ranger in Alaska fell into a crevasse and died on North America's tallest mountain.

item.title: Some House Republicans vote with Democrats to send more aid to Ukraine
item.description: The House of Representatives approved new funding for Ukraine after 18 Republicans broke with GOP leaders and voted with Democrats. Eleanor Mueller, Semafor White House economic policy reporter, and Igor Bobic, U.S. Senate reporter for NOTUS, join "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: White House ballroom donors won billions in new government contracts, watchdog group finds
item.description: As the legal battle over President Trump's ballroom plays out, a new report from the watchdog group Public Citizen found that ballroom donors won new $50 billion in new government contracts. In response, the White House called the contracts "fake conflicts of interest." Robert Weissman, the co-president of Public Citizen, joins "The Takeout" to discuss.

item.title: Who's really at your door? California lawmakers respond to app delivery driver fraud
item.description: Do you know who you're opening your door to? Lawmakers respond after a CBS California consumer investigation found food delivery drivers using rented or stolen app accounts to bypass background checks, exposing a loophole that could put customers at risk.

item.title: The job market is much stronger than economists expected. Why?
item.description: Americans say it's tough to find a job, but employers just added a surprisingly strong 172,000 new hires in May.

item.title: Breaking down California's ballot counting, elections
item.description: The wheels of democracy slowly turned in California on Friday as the U.S. continued to wait for the winner to be declared in two high-profile primaries. 3.5 million ballots have yet to be counted. CBS News election law expert and contributor David Becker has more.

item.title: FBI fires analysts who worked on memo about Catholic extremist ideology
item.description: The five fired FBI analysits were involved in the creation of a withdrawn internal 2023 intelligence memo on "Radical Traditionalist Catholic" ideology, sources said.

item.title: Colombia's Petro says "without a doubt" Trump is interfering with his country's election
item.description: Outgoing Colombian President Gustavo Petro told CBS News' Lilia Luciano that "without a doubt" President Trump is interfering in his country's election by endorsing right-wing candidate Abelardo De La Espriella. "Any interference by one country over another in order to determine the destiny of another is an attempt against freedom," Petro said.

item.title: American journalist speaks out from inside notorious Iranian prison
item.description: An Iranian-American journalist being held in Iran's notorious Evin Prison made a plea for help in a CBS News-obtained recording. "Face the Nation" moderator Margaret Brennan reports.

item.title: After deadly bus crash, investigators probe company's links to other carriers
item.description: The company that operated a bus involved in a deadly crash in Virginia last week has ties to a broader network of travel firms, including one shut down by regulators a decade ago, a CBS News investigation has found.

item.title: Graham Platner calls allegations "politically motivated"
item.description: Maine Democratic Senate candidate Graham Platner is holding a campaign rally on Friday, following a week marked by allegations. He pushed back against yesterday's New York Times report, where several former girlfriends accused him of "unsettling" behavior. CBS News' Taurean Small reports and Midcoast Villager deputy editor Alex Seitz-Wald has more.

item.title: DOJ confirms in court papers the "anti-weaponization fund" isn't going forward
item.description: In a pair of legal filings Friday, the Justice Department stated in writing for what appears to be the first time that a controversial $1.7 billion "anti-weaponization fund" will not continue.

item.title: 1 dead, 3 hurt in high school graduation shooting in Northern California
item.description: One person died and three others, including a child, were injured in a shooting Wednesday night at a high school graduation in Northern California, officials said.

item.title: First officer told captain of United Airlines flight that hit light pole he was "slow and a little low," NTSB says
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board released its preliminary report on a United Airlines plane that struck a light pole on the New Jersey Turnpike in May.

item.title: Analyzing what the May jobs report says about the economy
item.description: Job creation is not roaring in the U.S., but it is running well ahead of expectations. The economy created 172,000 jobs in May, employment data shows. CBS News MoneyWatch correspondent Kelly O'Grady has more.

item.title: Judge refers DOJ lawyers for possible discipline over handling of transgender care probe
item.description: U.S. District Judge Mary McElroy had lambasted Justice Department lawyers in a decision last month and accused them of misrepresenting and withholding information.

item.title: Space station crew briefly moves to "safe haven" amid concerns over leaks
item.description: Out of an abundance of caution, NASA briefly directed five of the seven crew members aboard the International Space Station to wait inside the docked SpaceX Crew Dragon "Freedom" spacecraft.

item.title: Tom Homan, Trump's border czar, reacts to abuse allegations at Delaney Hall ICE facility
item.description: Tom Homan, President Trump's border czar, is reacting to prolonged protests outside New Jersey's Delaney Hall ICE facility amid allegations of inhumane conditions for detained migrants. CBS News' Camilo Montoya-Galvez reports.

item.title: Actor James Handy allegedly killed by girlfriend's son at LA home
item.description: Michael Gledhill, 44, was arrested on suspicion of murder after he turned himself in following the fatal stabbing of Handy, the LAPD said.
